title: tragopogon ruthenicus
intro: Tragopogon ruthenicus, also known as salsify or goat's beard, is a perennial plant native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible root. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The root of the plant is rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes digestive health and has been linked to several health benefits, including improved immune function and reduced cholesterol levels. Tragopogon ruthenicus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
